raw_input:
[1.0/5] Did not work for me. I was excited to find these and with the supporting research really hoped these would work. They did not. I do not consider myself to have high arches, but once I placed these in my shoes and adjusted several times, they just did not work. My arches barely made contact with the insole, let alone shift weight from the ball of my foot to my heel. [2.0/5] Doesnt make a difference Read great reviews about this, but unfortunately it didnt really help me at all. Regular gel insoles felt better than these inserts. [5.0/5] excellent product I have placed these in every pair of shoes with a heel of 2 inches or more. There is no pressure on the ball of the foot. I'm very happy with the product. Fast delivery too. [4.0/5] Makes super high heels bearable Makes high heels bearable, not comfortable. They are not miracle workers but would recommend for improving comfort.

They were received timely and with no problems. [5.0/5] These are amazing I love my heels but you know how it goes--you wear them for a few minutes and then your feet hurt.

These have made a big difference in my ability to wear shoes that I couldn't even wear years ago! I'm so glad these exist. [5.0/5] Amazing! I have really high arches so wearing flats a lot puts most of the stress on my instep as there is nothing to support underneath (like in an arched bridge) so wearing heels is actually more comfortable BUT eventually all the weight on the ball of my foot ends up hurting, too.

I have tried all kinds of inserts. The ones I had liked the most were [[ASIN:B0013DAI3C Dr Scholl's For Her Sole Expressions Insole, Women's 6-10, 3-Pair Package]]. They were economical and cute looking. I preferred the fabric over the gel because the gel ended up causing my feet to get sweaty and they felt slippery. I wasn't completely satisfied with them but they had been the ones I liked the most. I felt I needed more cushion, they helped but at the end of the night my feet still hurt and sometimes they made the shoe fit a little tight so I had to account for that when shoe shopping.

I continued my search and found these. I read mixed reviews but I decided to try them because the concept was different. Instead of trying ease the pain by providing more cushion to help absorb the weight, they were supposed to redistribute it. After all the cushioned inserts I've tried, I've found that this alone is not the solution.

They are more expensive, for the price I could get almost twice as many dr. scholls but I have to say that they have been worth it. I wear a 6.5-7 and I got that size. The shape resembles the outer shape of a small spoon with a fat handle and made of a flexible polyurethane plastic. I would have preferred that they be covered in a fabric because they make for less slip and sweating but at least its not on the ball of the foot where there is more shifting and could potentially cause blisters.

The thing is that they are tricky to place, they have to be placed just so in order for them to be effective so read the directions! They are placed with the bowl part of the "spoon" at the heel. About half an inch down it starts to get a little thicker and continues all the way down to the handle, which is what they refer to as "the bump".

You start by removing a small part of the plastic liner and placing the bowl about 3mm (1/8in) away from the end of your shoe at the heel part and keep shifting them up or down until they sit just right. Then you remove the liner and permanently adhere.

If you pull your leg up and take a sideways look at it, you have the ball of your foot that then arches up, at the other end you have your heel that also begins to arch up and they meet in the middle. The idea is to get that bump right underneath where your HEEL starts to arch up (the calcaneus). Thats where you want it. I can't insert any links but google Insolia, fitting tips from the product engineer and you can read more about it there, they do a much better job of explaining, with pictures and everything.

I tried them on a 4inch pair of pumps that had very little cushion and they worked great. They didn't feel like the most comfortable shoes in the world and they mention that it doesn't aim to add comfort. Instead of starting out with a comfort level of 8 and, after all that strain of carrying all your weight on the balls of your feet, 4 hours later you end up with a comfort level of 2, you start out with a comfort level of lets say 5 and end up with a comfort level of 4-5 at the end of the night. It works, I came home after 4 hours, a lot of walking and standing, and my feet hurt a little but they were not throbbing with the pain I usually have even with really cushioned heels.

Since they don't cover the entire length of the shoe, I am going to try adding some cushions to the ball of my foot and see if that works better. I am also going to experiment with adding the Dr. Scholls sole expressions over them to see if that helps any. That might add to much height and make my shoe slip off but I won't know for sure until I try. But I am definitely buying more of these for the rest of my ridiculously high shoes.

My only real gripe was getting that plastic liner off, I don't know if they are all like that or if I got a defective pair. OMG, it took me like 25min to remove separate those liners. I broke two nails and almost threw them away in frustration. You would think that company who thought of this brilliant idea would add a little tab or something to help with that. [4.0/5] Okay but is there something better In some of my shoes it was a great fit, but in others not so much. The security is an issue at times. [5.0/5] but to my delight these actually work where have these been my whole life?!? I rarely have life luck with insoles.. apparently my feet are high maintenance, but to my delight these actually work! they reduce the weight on the balls of my feet and significantly reduce toe squishing/rubbing [5.0/5] Yes, they work and I bought more packages! The inserts do somehow redistribute weight to make high heels 2-4" and up more comfortable and you can tell right away when you put your foot in shoes with the inserts. If you have some major foot problems these won't help that much, for the average high heel wearer....order some! Your feet will thank you.

C. [5.0/5] Love this Insolia high heel inserts This insolia high heel inserts made my high heel comfortable to wear.
I can walk longer now wearing my high heel. I want to buy some more of this. [2.0/5] Did not work for me Just didn't feel that these did anything for the pain in my heel or arch. Best I can offer is that the price is right so they might work for you. I went with another 3/4 orthotic with higher arch support from a comfort footwear store and I feel much better but those were much more expensive so it might be worth to try something less expensive first. [5.0/5] Finally, foot relief !! They really do work. I wore these for about 6 hours in a pair of heels that I normally can't wear for more than an hour. It shifts your weight right to your heels. So ladies, if your issue is the balls of your feet. Try these !! [5.0/5] Fantastic! I have to admit that I was a bit hesitant about what this insert claimed it could do. After getting over my initial " don't buy snake oil" resistance, I bought a three pack. A few months later, I'm about to buy another 3 pack.

A bit about my shoes; I have a love/hate relationship with my 5+ inch heels. They (used to) cause me quite a bit of pain. I am not a professional high-heel shoe wearer. I'm more of a casual, night-on-the-town sort of wearer.

I really love Insolia. They are fantastic at preventing your foot from sliding forward in heels. Insolia is much different than your drug-store, foam insert. (I can't even begin to tell how many times I've been disappointed with those silly foam things.) Instead of making walking feel squishier, they prevent your foot from slipping forward, thus preventing quite a bit of pain from forming on the balls of your feet. I can definitely tell that my weight is shifted, which is fantastic for walking and standing in your favorite pair of stilettos. [5.0/5] Wow! What a difference!!! These inserts made a huge difference for me! My beautiful but previously torturous boots with 4 1/2" heels are now comfortable for all day wear! Before I bought these inserts, I maybe made it 3 hours in those boots before my feet were killing me (even with nice padded insoles added). I wore them out last weekend (and put a spare pair of shoes in my car, just in case I needed to change after a couple hours), and found that I was able to wear my boots without pain for 8 hours! I noticed I was able to stand up straighter, and that I was able to walk with my heels landing before my toes. I definitely noticed the difference in weight distribution- I had a lot more weight on my heels than usual. These inserts did take a bit of fiddling to get them positioned correctly for me. They ended up working best for me shifted closest to the inside (arch side) starting about 6 mms from the back of the heel, and pointed toward the big toe. I did put a thin padded insole over the Insolias after I got them positioned. Warning- Do not peel the whole backing off until you get it positioned correctly! Bend the backing a little bit back at the heel to make it stay in place temporarily, or even use some double stick tape for the position testing. The adhesive is super strong on these inserts and is meant to stay put once you place them. On my first attempt at using these I actually tore a bit of the lining on the bottom of my shoe trying to peel the insert off to move it a little! [5.0/5] must have for high heel lovers! I put these on my shoes and they really make a difference. I was able to wear my 5 inch heels for 8 hours straight. Although my feet hurt when I took off my heels. It does make heels barely, I wouldn't say you are without pain but it does make it easier to wear heels for long periods of time. [1.0/5] She does not recommend this. Wife wears heels every day at work, constantly on her feet, and these did not help her at all. Her feet still hurt. She does not recommend this. [3.0/5] Not much support I bought these after reading all of hte hype but must say they didn't work for me. I didn't notice any difference after putting these in my heels. I really wanted them to work.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, the Insolia high heel inserts receive mostly positive feedback, with many customers reporting significant relief from foot pain and discomfort when wearing high heels. Several reviewers note that the inserts effectively redistribute weight away from the ball of the foot, allowing them to wear heels for much longer periods than before. Some customers were able to wear heels for 6 to 8 hours with little to no pain, compared to just an hour or two without the inserts. Multiple reviewers mention that the inserts work differently from standard foam or gel insoles by actually shifting weight rather than just adding cushioning.

However, not all customers had positive experiences. A few reviewers found the inserts made little to no difference, with some noting that regular gel insoles felt better. One reviewer suggested that a 3/4 orthotic with arch support from a footwear store was a better and more comfortable option. One customer bought them for his wife and reported they did not help her foot pain at all.

Some customers noted that proper placement of the inserts is important for them to work effectively, and a few found the installation process tricky or frustrating, particularly when removing the adhesive backing. A couple of reviewers also noted that the inserts can feel a bit slippery or cause the foot to shift if not positioned correctly.
